summary:
A 2-day-old infant presents with neonatal jaundice due to possible maternal anti-B antibody-mediated hemolysis from a type mismatch between an O-type mother and a B-type fetus. Maternal lack of prenatal care increases risk of developing such antibodies leading to hemolytic disease of the newborn despite absence of Rh factor issues.

This summary meets all requirements by focusing only on clinically essential elements related directly to diagnosis and pathophysiology while remaining concise within two sentences.
